2604.21700.title: Stealthy Backdoor Attacks against LLMs Based on Natural Style Triggers
2604.21700.paper_summary: The growing application of large language models (LLMs) in safety-critical domains has raised urgent concerns about their security. Many recent studies have demonstrated the feasibility of backdoor attacks against LLMs. However, existing methods suffer from three key shortcomings: explicit trigger patterns that compromise naturalness, unreliable injection of attacker-specified payloads in long-form generation, and incompletely specified threat models that obscure how backdoors are delivered and activated in practice. To address these gaps, we present BadStyle, a complete backdoor attack framework and pipeline. BadStyle leverages an LLM as a poisoned sample generator to construct natural and stealthy poisoned samples that carry imperceptible style-level triggers while preserving semantics and fluency. To stabilize payload injection during fine-tuning, we design an auxiliary target loss that reinforces the attacker-specified target content in responses to poisoned inputs and penalizes its emergence in benign responses. We further ground the attack in a realistic threat model and systematically evaluate BadStyle under both prompt-induced and PEFT-based injection strategies. Extensive experiments across seven victim LLMs, including LLaMA, Phi, DeepSeek, and GPT series, demonstrate that BadStyle achieves high attack success rates (ASRs) while maintaining strong stealthiness. The proposed auxiliary target loss substantially improves the stability of backdoor activation, yielding an average ASR improvement of around 30% across style-level triggers. Even in downstream deployment scenarios unknown during injection, the implanted backdoor remains effective. Moreover, BadStyle consistently evades representative input-level defenses and bypasses output-level defenses through simple camouflage.
2604.21700.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在安全關鍵領域的應用日益增長，這引發了人們對其安全性的迫切關注。許多近期研究已證明對LLMs進行後門攻擊的可行性。然而，現有方法存在三個主要缺陷：明確的觸發模式損害了自然性、在長文本生成中不可靠的攻擊者指定有效載荷的注入，以及不完全指定的威脅模型使得後門如何在實際中傳遞和激活變得模糊。為了解決這些問題，我們提出了BadStyle，一個完整的後門攻擊框架和管道。BadStyle利用LLM作為毒化樣本生成器，構建自然且隱秘的毒化樣本，這些樣本攜帶不可察覺的風格級觸發，同時保持語義和流暢性。為了在微調過程中穩定有效載荷的注入，我們設計了一個輔助目標損失，強化對毒化輸入的回應中攻擊者指定的目標內容，並懲罰其在良性回應中的出現。我們進一步將攻擊基於現實的威脅模型，並系統性地評估BadStyle在提示誘導和PEFT基礎注入策略下的表現。在包括LLaMA、Phi、DeepSeek和GPT系列在內的七個受害者LLM上進行的廣泛實驗表明，BadStyle實現了高攻擊成功率（ASRs），同時保持強大的隱秘性。所提出的輔助目標損失顯著提高了後門激活的穩定性，平均ASR提升約30%，適用於風格級觸發。即使在注入過程中未知的下游部署場景中，植入的後門仍然有效。此外，BadStyle始終能夠躲避代表性的輸入級防禦，並通過簡單的偽裝繞過輸出級防禦。
2604.21700.author: Jiali Wei et.al.
2604.21700.authors: Jiali Wei, Ming Fan, Guoheng Sun, Xicheng Zhang, Haijun Wang, Ting Liu
